title: Darth Maleval

{{Quote|I don't know what frightened me more—the different ways he found to kill…or the joy they gave him.|Anson Trask|<ref name="leg4" />}}
'''Darth Maleval''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] of the [[One Sith]], serving under [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] and his [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]], after Krayt [[Attack on Emperor Fel|deposed]] [[Fel Empire|Galactic]] [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]. By [[137 ABY]], many units, such as the [[908th Stormtrooper Division]], had defected to [[Empire-in-exile|Emperor-in-exile]] Fel during the [[Second Imperial Civil War]]. To ensure the loyalty of Krayt's [[Troop/Legends|troops]], Maleval was given command of the [[407th Stormtrooper Division]], which was [[Imperial base (Yinchorr)|based]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], as they attempted to recapture the [[908th's Headquarters|base]] of the 908th on [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]].

When the [[Battle of Borosk (137 ABY)|Battle of Borosk]] broke out, Maleval led the 407th [[Division/Legends|division]] in recapturing the base and ensuring the complete destruction of the 908th down to the last [[Stormtrooper/Legends|man]]. After the battle, Maleval ordered [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Gil Cassel]] of the 407th to kill his brother, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Jared Cassel]] of the 908th, who had defected to Fel. Maleval [[Death/Legends|killed]] both Cassel brothers when Gil refused, causing trooper [[Hondo Karr]], of the 407th's [[Joker Squad]], to attack Maleval. Before he could execute Karr, Maleval was shot and killed from behind by another trooper, [[Anson Trask]]. The surviving members of Joker Squad covered up the event in the report to their superiors, stating that Maleval had been killed in battle, and that Karr was vaporized in an explosion, allowing Karr to leave the Empire and later join the unified fragments of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] military, the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]].

==Biography==
===Commanding the 407th===
{{Quote|See to his disciplining. Or I ''shall''.|Darth Maleval warns Sergeant Ran Harkas to punish Anson Trask|<ref name="leg4" />}}
[[File:Maleval.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Darth Maleval on Yinchorr]]
The [[Quarren/Legends|Quarren]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Maleval was a member of the [[One Sith]], serving [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]] and his [[The galaxy/Legends|Galactic]] [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]], after Krayt [[Attack on Emperor Fel|overthrew]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] at the end of the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. In [[137 ABY]], during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Second Imperial Civil War]], Maleval was chosen to command the [[407th Stormtrooper Division]]. [[Imperial base (Yinchorr)|Stationed]] on [[Yinchorr/Legends|Yinchorr]], the 407th [[Division/Legends|Division]] was charged with recapturing the [[908th's Headquarters|base]] of the [[908th Stormtrooper Division]] on [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] and destroying the division itself, which had defected to Fel's [[Empire-in-exile]]. Given the defections, Krayt was uncertain of the 407th's loyalty to his Empire, and he sent Maleval to ensure that they did their duty.<ref name="leg4" />

After arriving on Yinchorr, Maleval was talking with the leaders of the 407th when he was approached by [[Stormtrooper/Legends|trooper]] [[Anson Trask]], who was looking for his new unit, [[Joker Squad]]. When Maleval turned around, Trask—shocked by the Quarren's appearance—angered the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] by uttering the expletive, "Sith me." Just ten days out of training, Trask was saved from Maleval's wrath by [[Sergeant/Legends|Sergeant]] [[Ran Harkas]] of Joker Squad, who excused Trask, explaining that he was a "[[noob]]" and that no disrespect was intended. Maleval warned Harkas to make sure the [[Trooper/Legends|trooper]] was disciplined, or he would do it himself.<ref name="leg4" />

===Battle of Borosk===
{{Quote|There will be no truce. There will be no surrender. I don't care how many die. An example will be made of the deserters. The 908th are ''traitors'', and will be ''killed''—every last one.|Darth Maleval, during the Battle of Borosk|<ref name="leg4" />}}
To begin the [[Battle of Borosk (137 ABY)|attack]] on Borosk, Maleval led a vanguard, which included Joker Squad, in capturing and securing [[Artillery/Legends|gun]] and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] placements along the perimeter wall of the 908th's headquarters. During the battle, he wielded his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] in one hand and his [[shock whip]] in the other, cutting down stormtroopers without caring whether they were of the 908th or his own. He found many different ways to kill his enemies, and took great joy in their [[Death/Legends|deaths]].<ref name="leg4" />

After the 407th had succeeded in capturing the base's gun and shield platforms and had stood down for the night, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Gil Cassel]] of the 407th suggested that a truce be made with the 908th to give them a chance to surrender. Hearing of this, Maleval approached him about his decision. Maleval had learned that Cassel had a [[Jared Cassel|brother]] in the 908th; however, Cassel assured the Sith Lord that his brother was not the reason he had suggested the truce, and that it was to prevent needless loss of lives and matériel. Maleval told the lieutenant that he did not care how many died—there would be no truce or surrender, an example would be made of the 908th, and they would all be killed.<ref name="leg4" />

[[File:Jared_Cassel.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Darth Maleval dragged Jared Cassel by the neck to present to Gil Cassel.]]
He informed Joker Squad that they would be the ones to lead the attack on the 908th, warning them that he would be watching and that if they faltered in the slightest, he would kill them himself. In response to this, Joker Squad member [[Hondo Karr]] threatened the Sith Lord. Karr held his [[Knife/Legends|knife]] up at Maleval, who simply [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force-pushed]] him into a nearby wall. The encounter with Maleval brought about a debate among the members of Joker Squad. That night, options such as defecting, or permitting the 908th to escape, were discussed among the Jokers. Cassel was undecided when asked what they should do; however, Sergeant Harkas convinced them all to do their duty and complete the mission.<ref name="leg4" />

The next day, Maleval oversaw the attack on the base, with Joker Squad breaching the main fortress at about midday. After the battle was over, Maleval put a rope around the neck of Gil Cassel's captured brother, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Jared Cassel, and brought the defector before Gil. To prove his loyalty, Maleval ordered Gil to kill his own brother or stand condemned himself. Jared told Gil to do it as they should not both die, but Gil refused, saying that there were some things more important than duty or life. For Gil's refusal, Maleval condemned them as traitors and killed both of the brothers with his lightsaber.<ref name="leg4" />

[[File:Death_Maleval.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The death of Maleval on Borosk]]
Angered by the act, Hondo Karr once again attacked the Sith Lord, but Maleval stopped him with a backhand. Maleval then gripped Karr with his shock whip and began to electrocute him. Before he could execute Karr, however, Maleval was shot from behind by Anson Trask, ending the Sith Lord's life.<ref name="leg4" />

The surviving members of Joker Squad covered up the Maleval incident in a report to their superiors, stating that Maleval had been killed in battle, and that Hondo Karr was vaporized in a blast that took out fellow Joker Squad trooper [[Jes Gistang]], allowing Karr to leave the Empire and later join the unified fragments of the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] military, the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]].<ref name="leg4" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|I will be watching. If you falter in the ''slightest'', I will kill you all myself.|Darth Maleval, to Joker Squad|<ref name="leg4" />}}
Darth Maleval was a ruthless member of the One Sith, chosen by Darth Krayt to ensure the loyalty of the Empire's stormtroopers. He was a male Quarren with [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and like many other members of the Order, Maleval's face and visible parts of his body were covered in red and black [[Sith tattoo]]s. Maleval was aggressive and cared little for those who served under him, willingly killing both enemies and allies in battle. He had no compassion for his foes, wanting them dead to the last man rather than offering them the chance to surrender. In battle he was merciless, finding different ways to kill his victims and taking joy in their deaths.<ref name="leg4" /> He preferred to use the shock whip in combat, as it was more painful but less lethal, and could be used to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] his enemies for long periods of time before killing them. Maleval had even been known to leave his adversaries alive, but severely maimed, to serve as a warning to those who would oppose the Empire.<ref name="LECG">''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]''</ref> He was also cruel to those under his command, ordering Lieutenant Gil Cassel to kill his own brother to prove his loyalty, and killing both when he refused.<ref name="leg4" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|Do you think a Lord of the Sith dies so easily?|Darth Maleval, in response to Hondo Karr's attack|<ref name="leg4" />}}
[[File:Darth_Maleval_LECG.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Maleval utilizing his shock whip against a GA soldier]]
Maleval was a skilled [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|wielder]] of both the lightsaber and shock whip, often using both in battle to devastating effect—he was able to cut his way through the stormtroopers of the 908th with ease. He would use his shock whip to grip his opponents and electrocute them, taking pleasure from the pain it caused. Apart from his skill with melee weapons, Maleval was strong in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], capable of using telekinesis to push enemies with a powerful wave of Force energy. He could also [[Force sense/Legends|sense]] incoming attacks and use that knowledge to [[Precognition/Legends|react]] accordingly, as seen when Hondo Karr attacked the Sith Lord. Despite his ability with the Force, Maleval was unable to sense the impending attack by stormtrooper Anson Trask until it was too late, leading to the Sith Lord's death.<ref name="leg4" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Darth Maleval was created by writer [[John Ostrander]] and first appeared in the stand-alone fourth issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' series, [[Legacy (2006) 4|''Noob'']].<ref name="leg4" />

On the cover of ''Noob'', Maleval has five suction-tipped fingers on each hand, while in the comic he has three clawed fingers on each hand instead.<ref name="leg4" /> Maleval is the second of three Quarren Sith Lords portrayed in ''Star Wars: Legacy'', following an [[unidentified Quarren Sith]]<ref name="leg1">[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]]</ref> and preceding [[Darth Azard]].<ref name="leg20">[[Legacy (2006) 20|''Legacy'' (2006) 20]]</ref>

An Entertainment Earth exclusive action figure of Darth Maleval was unveiled at [[Wikipedia:San Diego Comic-Con International|Comic Con International 2008]] as part of [[Hasbro]]'s ''[[Star Wars: The Legacy Collection]]'' Joker Squad six-pack.<ref name="EE">{{WebCite|url=http://www.entertainmentearth.com/prodinfo.asp?number=HS87923|text=Star Wars Joker Squad Stormtrooper Figures - An EE Exclusive|work=Entertainmentearth.com|archivedate=20170611043813}}</ref><ref name="Hasbro">{{Hasbro|subdomain=www|url=starwars/en_US/discover/news/article.cfm?newsid=790B61C9-D56F-E112-49F5DA1F61997DE5|text=Exclusive Star Wars Joker Squad Available at Entertainment Earth}}</ref>
